# Transcrição: Clay Collins - Choosing a niche

- **Fonte:** Clay Collins - Choosing a niche
- **Duração:** 52:52

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey folks, it's Jeff and I'm very happy to have Clay Collins on the line with me. So Clay is a friend of mine. He's also a very, very sharp marketer and he's actually one of my mastermind partners. He's in a mastermind group with me and so that's how I actually initially got to know Clay and he's got lots of great talents and skills online but the one we want to talk about today is something that he's really good at is finding niches or more, I guess, more accurately helping people find their niche or a niche that will work for them. And so without any further ado, Clay, thank you very much for joining us. Very excited to be talking to you. Jeff, it's super great to be here. Thanks for having me. So I guess what we're going to do here is a lot of product launch formula owners have, they're just starting a new business or they're starting a new new business. They've got one but they want to go in a different direction. They're either looking for a niche or they're trying to figure out something, they've got a niche in mind and they're trying to figure out is that a good one? Is it big enough? Is it too big? Is it going to be profitable? And so I know this is something that you've got a lot of skill at and you've also, you just conceptually, it's a great way of approaching it and I know you can bring great value to them and so I'm just sort of going to hand it over to you and say, like, just where should we start with this? Well, you know what I think a good place to start is to realize that this really doesn't have to be as complicated as most people make it out to be. I think a lot of people when they first get into internet marketing or they're looking to start their second business, they kind of lock themselves in a room and they're looking at keyword research and pay-per-click numbers and like all this data and it becomes, it quickly can become this rocket scientist thing that just gets blown way out of proportion and it's almost like you have to resort to quantum physics to like try and figure out what kind of market to pursue online but it really, really, really doesn't have to be that complex at all. In fact, there's a lot of ways to go about market selection that are like much better than looking at numbers. It's just much simpler. So I think, you know, what I always like to tell people before they start looking at markets is that they really should draw like, you know, make a Venn diagram if they will at least one in their head and in one circle place all the things that they like to do or at least that they're passionate about and in another circle kind of take stock of the things that can make them money and then find that intersection, find that intersection
